texto: Photography is like stealing.You rob someone of a moment that exposes something essential about their character,their soul if you like.there are people who are very conscious of that,who find that terrifying.The thought that everyone,friend of foe,can get so close to you,look you straight in the eye and judge you without having any control over it or being able to respond.A part of them has become the property of the photographer.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Esther Verhoef: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Photography is like stealing.You rob someone of a moment that exposes something essential about t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, art, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e vida.

texto: You arrange your life based on what "everyone" thinks of you or what you think they think of you,whoever everyone is...

texto: There is too much pain in the world,in all shapes and forms.That is what these people represent.Pain from losing their homes,the death of a loved one,failing an exam,having a leg amputated,not getting a resident permit after years of hoping for a better life...That's what binds them,That's what they potray.Pain.

texto: It has struck me that people seldom listen to the meaning of underlying words.There are only a few people who recognize the silence beyond the scream,the gentle weeping that hides beyond tough statements.They hear only the words,the sentences,when there is so much more to take in.People who are too self-absorbed to notice,really see or fathom others.Often,they don't even know themselves.
explicacao: Citação de Esther Verhoef sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “It has struck me that people seldom listen to the meaning of underlying words.There are only a fe…” articula reflexao, vida, people em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e people.

texto: Wonderful art can spring from misery,I'm the last person to deny that.I'd go even further:the best works of art of all time are probably stemmed from the deep human sorrow or hellish frustration,the death of a loved one or a divorce and yes:jealousy.Heartache and impotence as the man-spring for making the unverifiable verifiable and for giving it face.How romantic,beautiful and especially useful pain and misery can be.

texto: True freedom only exists in art.The problem is you have to be incredibly good.No,I am putting it wrong:You don't so much have to be outstanding,what you really need are connections,if you don't want to be dependent on all kinds of government foundations who will impose their ideas and restrictions on you in turn.It can be touch and go in the beginning:It takes guts to let go of everything.But you make it,you're free.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Esther Verhoef sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “True freedom only exists in art.The problem is you have to be incredibly good.No,I am putting it …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, art, close-up, útil em momentos de termino e close-up.
